commands.profile: rocprofv3 --kernel-trace -d /tmp/hipengine-gguf-tuning/20260616-llamacpp-hip-q4km-pp512-warm/rocprof -o llamacpp-q4km-pp512-warm -f csv -- env HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 /home/lhl/llama.cpp/llama.cpp-hip/build/bin/llama-bench -fa 1 -m /models/gguf/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf -p 512 -n 0 -r 1 -o json -dev ROCm0

hardware.gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX, gfx1100, HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1

interpretation: The measured pp512 pass is dominated by llama.cpp mul_mat_q MMQ kernels: Q4_K type12 36.3%, Q5_K type13 17.3%, Q8_0 type8 15.2%, Q6_K type14 1.9%; with quantize_mmq_q8_1 included, MMQ/Q8_1 activation work is about 72.9% of kernel time.
Flash attention is only 1.6% of the measured pass kernel time at pp512, so the short-prefill lead is not primarily an attention-kernel issue.
This supports testing a llama.cpp-style Q8_1 activation + MMQ tile path for hipEngine GGUF prefill before more selected-WMMA live-state tweaks.

profile_method.measured_pass_detection: The profiled command includes one warmup pp512 pass plus one measured pp512 pass. The measured pass is summarized as all dispatches after the first mul_mat_vec_q lm-head dispatch, which terminates the warmup pass.

reason_not_promoted: External-baseline profile only; no hipEngine runtime code changed or performance improvement claimed.
summary: llama.cpp HIP Qwen3.6-35B-A3B Q4_K_M pp512 rocprofv3 kernel-family capture on GPU1.
